decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of card advantage, removal spells, and countermagic. Utilizing cards like Brainstorm and Ponder to dig for answers, alongside disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to stop opponent's threats. The deck also includes efficient removal like Fatal Push and Witherbloom Command, as well as creature threats like Uro and Baleful Strix to apply pressure. The mana base is carefully crafted to support the deck's strategy, with utility lands like Wasteland and Undercity Sewers to disrupt opponents.

The deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with powerful creatures, then using spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead to bring them back onto the battlefield for a strong board presence. This strategy relies on disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will while efficiently searching for combo pieces with Brainstorm and Ponder. A combination of control elements and powerful reanimation spells allows the deck to quickly overwhelm opponents with big threats.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources through denial tactics like Sphere of Resistance and Wasteland while setting up a powerful win condition with the Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage combo. It utilizes lands like Urza's Saga and Maze of Ith to generate value and protect its own threats. Life from the Loam provides long-term card advantage and helps ensure consistent land drops. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while assembling its own win condition to secure victory.

This Mardu Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature strategies and disruption spells to control the battlefield. The key concept of the deck is to quickly establish a board presence with efficient creatures like Amped Raptor and Ocelot Pride, supported by removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and disruption like Thoughtseize and Cabal Therapy. The deck also includes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Goblin Bombardment for additional reach and damage output. The inclusion of Jegantha, the Wellspring as a companion provides an additional powerful option for late-game value.

This deck focuses on aggressively disrupting the opponent's game plan while using powerful creatures and equipment to close out the game quickly. By utilizing a mix of disruptive creatures like Thalia and Skyclave Apparition along with efficient removal like Swords to Plowshares, the deck aims to control the board while applying pressure. Stoneforge Mystic and Aether Vial provide additional flexibility and consistency, allowing for explosive plays and versatile responses to threats. Additionally, the inclusion of Yorion as a companion adds a long-term advantage by generating card advantage and enabling recurring value.

This deck is centered around milling your entire library and winning the game with Thassa's Oracle on the battlefield. The deck utilizes a variety of fast mana sources and graveyard recursion spells to quickly fill the graveyard with combo pieces. The key strategy is to use cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to mill the entire library, then use Thassa's Oracle with little to no cards in the library to win the game on the spot. The deck also includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Pact of Negation to protect the combo.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful creatures like Atraxa and Archon of Cruelty to quickly reanimate them from the graveyard, while controlling the game with disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will. It also includes card draw and filtering to help find key pieces of the combo. The mana base is built to support the deck's color requirements and includes utility lands like Wasteland. The goal is to quickly bring back game-winning creatures and overwhelm the opponent before they can react, creating a powerful and explosive strategy.

The deck focuses on using Stifle and Phyrexian Dreadnought to control the opponent's plays and prevent them from gaining momentum. It also includes powerful control cards like Brainstorm, Force of Will, and Swords to Plowshares to disrupt the opponent's strategies. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Curie, Emergent Intelligence provide card advantage and additional control options. The mana base is optimized for consistency and includes key lands like Wasteland and Karakas. Overall, the deck aims to efficiently control the game and overpower the opponent with carefully timed and strategic plays.

This deck aims to control the game through a combination of creature removal, counterspells, and card draw. The key strategy revolves around using powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to generate card advantage and out-value the opponent. Additionally, the deck includes powerful threats like Uro, Titan of Nature's Wrath to apply pressure and close out the game. The mana base is optimized to support the deck's multiple colors and ensure consistent access to its powerful spells. Overall, the deck seeks to outlast opponents and dominate the late game with its powerful control elements.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling the Aluren combo to cheat giant creatures like Emrakul onto the battlefield. It uses a combination of cheap creatures, cantrips, and counterspells to protect its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The goal is to ramp up mana with cards like Ancient Tomb and Lotus Petal, then cast Show and Tell or Aluren to put powerful creatures into play for free. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with huge threats while maintaining control of the game through countermagic and card advantage.

The key concept of this deck is to quickly assemble artifacts on the battlefield, taking advantage of their synergies and abilities to put pressure on the opponent and disrupt their strategy. By using cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Urza's Saga, the deck aims to generate incremental advantage and maintain a fast tempo. The inclusion of cards like Chalice of the Void and Pithing Needle helps control the opponent's plays, while cards like Force of Will provide protection against opposing threats. Overall, the deck focuses on aggressive artifact-based gameplay and efficient resource management.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on fast-paced creature aggression, utilizing cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Amped Raptor to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes disruption elements like Swords to Plowshares and Static Prison to control the board. Additionally, there are land destruction cards like Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base. With a mix of powerful creatures and disruptive spells, this deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and maintain a relentless assault to secure victory.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy while quickly applying pressure with efficient creatures and counter spells. It utilizes aggressive creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Barrowgoyf, along with disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to disrupt the opponent's plans. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Murktide Regent provide card advantage and additional threats. The deck aims to control the game tempo, managing the board state while steadily closing out the game. Efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out help maintain control of the battlefield.

This deck focuses on quickly establishing board control and disrupting the opponent's strategy while deploying evasive threats to close out the game. By efficiently utilizing cards like Brainstorm, Daze, and Thoughtseize, the deck is able to maintain tempo and pressure the opponent. Murktide Regent and Barrowgoyf provide powerful threats that are difficult to deal with, while Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student offers card advantage and utility. The deck's mana base is streamlined for consistency, with a focus on blue and black sources to support its disruptive spells and evasive creatures.

The main idea of this deck is to use Show and Tell to put powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Atraxa, Grand Unifier onto the battlefield for free. By using cards like Omniscience and Stock Up, the deck can generate a lot of value and potentially win the game in a single turn. The deck also includes counter magic like Force of Will and Veil of Summer to protect its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan. Additionally, cards like Cunning Wish provide versatility and access to silver bullet cards from the sideboard.

This Control - Stoneblade deck focuses on utilizing a combination of powerful creatures and disruptive spells to control the pace of the game and outvalue the opponent. By utilizing cards like Stoneforge Mystic to fetch equipment like Batterskull and Quantum Riddler to provide card advantage, along with a suite of permission spells like Force of Will and disruptive spells like Stifle and Daze, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying threats of its own to close out the game.
